notes: Sintered silicon carbide (SiC) armor ceramic. Density 3.15-3.25 g/cm³, Knoop hardness ~2500 HK (25 GPa). Standalone RHAe ~3.0-4.0 per mm vs KE — harder than Al2O3 but more expensive. Used in modern lightweight composite arrays (Leopard 2A5+, Challenger 2 TES). Superior multi-hit capability vs B4C due to higher fracture toughness K1c ~3.5-4.5 MPa·m^½. Lower density than Al2O3 for weight-critical applications.

source.reference: Ceramic armor manufacturer datasheets (CoorsTek, Ceradyne), ARL research
source.methodology: RHAe from published ballistic test data on sintered SiC vs AP, normalized to RHA. Density and fracture toughness from manufacturer specifications.
